description: Controversial rule questionnaire consumed by the gum onboarding script and the bubbletea TUI. Each question maps to one or more rules and a per-profile default answer. Parent questions cascade to children via the cascade_from field; when a parent is answered, cascaded children are skipped in the user-facing flow and their answers are derived from the parent.

questions.question: Enforce strict null safety?
questions.rationale: Bans the !! operator and flags every unsafe cast. Chooses a hard stance on nullability at the cost of some idiomatic patterns like requireNotNull()!! chains.

questions.question: Allow the !! (not-null assertion) operator?
questions.rationale: !! throws NPE on null. Some teams ban it entirely; others consider it idiomatic for known-nonnull patterns.

questions.question: Flag unsafe casts (as T)?
questions.rationale: `as T` throws ClassCastException on mismatch. Teams that prefer `as?` + explicit null handling want this flagged.

questions.question: Flag magic numbers?
questions.rationale: MagicNumber is the highest-volume style rule in most codebases. Teams that heavily use Compose / resource IDs / scaling factors often find this churn rather than signal.

questions.question: Flag TODO / FIXME / STOPSHIP comments?
questions.rationale: Some teams treat TODOs as tickets-in-code and want CI to fail on them. Others use them as permanent documentation of known limitations.

questions.question: Flag wildcard imports (import foo.*)?
questions.rationale: Wildcard imports hide what the file actually depends on. IntelliJ auto-collapses imports beyond a threshold, so large files can end up with wildcards even when the team wants explicit imports.

questions.question: Flag unused parameters and private properties?
questions.rationale: Helps find dead code. Noisy on interface implementations and Compose preview parameters — the rule respects `ignored|expected` naming but still fires on other unused params.

questions.question: Flag variable name shadowing?
questions.rationale: Shadowing `val x` inside a `let { x -> ... }` block is legal but error-prone. Common in DSLs and builders, where teams may want it allowed.

questions.question: Flag the spread operator (*args)?
questions.rationale: The spread operator allocates a new array on every call. Hot paths prefer explicit varargs; tests prefer the convenience.

questions.question: Enforce Compose stability?
questions.rationale: Flag @Composable functions with unstable parameters (List, Map, Set without @Immutable). Catches unnecessary recomposition but requires kotlinx.collections.immutable to be adopted across the codebase first, otherwise every function fires.

questions.question: Flag unstable @Composable parameters?
questions.rationale: Flags `List<T>` / `Map<K,V>` / `Set<T>` parameters that force recomposition. Use kotlinx.collections.immutable's `ImmutableList` etc. to fix.

questions.question: Flag lambdas capturing unstable state in composition?
questions.rationale: Lambdas that capture MutableState or unstable types cause recomposition. Usually paired with flag-unstable-compose-params as a set of Compose stability rules.

questions.question: Flag mutable default arguments in @Composable functions?
questions.rationale: A mutable default like `list: MutableList<T> = mutableListOf()` is shared across all call sites and causes state leaks.

questions.question: Enforce dispatcher injection?
questions.rationale: Require tests to override dispatchers via DI rather than hard-coding Dispatchers.IO / Default / Unconfined. Also bans GlobalScope usage. Essential for deterministic coroutine tests.

questions.question: Flag GlobalScope.launch / GlobalScope.async?
questions.rationale: GlobalScope coroutines escape structured concurrency and leak across test boundaries. Teams without a coroutine test harness may still use it.

questions.question: Enforce DI scope hygiene (Anvil / Hilt)?
questions.rationale: Catches common DI mistakes: Anvil @ContributesBinding without a scope, @MergeComponent with an empty scope list, @Binds with mismatched arity, @EntryPoint on a non-interface.
